matchup: San Antonio Spurs at Oklahoma City Thunder

clv_and_market_read.favorite_inflation: OKC closed as heavy favorite despite matchup signs showing SAS could survive through glass control and Wembanyama gravity.
clv_and_market_read.total_mispricing: Closing total near 219-220 undershot the actual 237 by roughly 16.5-18.5 points. Double overtime inflated final, but regulation still reached 216, keeping the total market tight even before OT.
clv_and_market_read.team_total_truth: Both standard team totals were low relative to final output. SAS cleared by 15.5-16.5 points; OKC cleared 112.5/113.5 but missed the inflated Kalshi 115.5 by half-point.
clv_and_market_read.market_blind_spot: Books priced OKC superiority but did not fully price the SAS possession engine: +20 rebound margin, +7 offensive rebounds, and +7 free-throw point edge.

model_alignment.closing_line_implication_for_game2: Market may correct OKC price downward or total upward, but if OKC is still priced as a clean -6.5/-7 favorite, SAS plus-points remains live unless lineup/foul/turnover corrections materially shift.

SAS_team_total.preliminary_edge: SAS TT over has raw model edge, but requires checking whether market reflects Game 1 OT scoring tax and OKC defensive correction.

OKC_team_total.preliminary_edge: OKC TT over is playable only at <=113.5; Kalshi 115.5 +119 is thin/high-volatility and not a priority.

full_game_total.preliminary_edge: If Game 2 total opens/holds below 224.5, over remains in attack range; above 229.5 becomes price-sensitive.
